preamble: The shift from a voiceless palatal stop [c] to an alveolar sibilant [s] is a common path in the development of Romance languages, typically occurring through an intermediate affricate stage [ts].

examples.note: Historical palatalization of Latin [k] before front vowels led to [c], then [ts], and finally [s] in French.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
